sn35.95:1.1: Then Ven. Māluṅkyaputta, who was ardent & resolute, went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side. As he was sitting there, he said to the Blessed One:
sn35.95:1.2: “It would be good, lord, if the Blessed One would teach me the Dhamma in brief so that, having heard the Dhamma from the Blessed One, I might dwell alone in seclusion: heedful, ardent, & resolute.”
sn35.95:2.1: “Here now, Māluṅkyaputta: What will I say
sn35.95:2.2: to the young monks when you—aged, old, elderly, along in years, come to the last stage of life—ask for an admonition in brief?”
sn35.95:3.1: “Lord, even though I’m aged, old, elderly, along in years, come to the last stage of life,
sn35.95:3.2: may the Blessed One teach me the Dhamma in brief! May the One Well-Gone teach me the Dhamma in brief! It may well be that I’ll understand the Blessed One’s words. It may well be that I’ll become an heir to the Blessed One’s words.”
sn35.95:4.2: The forms cognizable via the eye that are unseen by you—that you have never before seen, that you don’t see, and that are not to be seen by you: Do you have any desire or passion or love there?”
sn35.95:8.1: “The tactile sensations cognizable via the body…
sn35.95:9.1: “The ideas cognizable via the intellect that are uncognized by you–the you have never before cognized, that you don’t cognize, and that are not to be cognized by you: Do you have any desire or passion or love there?”
sn35.95:10.1: “Then, Māluṅkyaputta, with regard to phenomena to be seen, heard, sensed, or cognized: In reference to the seen, there will be only the seen. In reference to the heard, only the heard. In reference to the sensed, only the sensed. In reference to the cognized, only the cognized.
sn35.95:10.2: That is how you should train yourself. When for you there will be only the seen in reference to the seen, only the heard in reference to the heard, only the sensed in reference to the sensed, only the cognized in reference to the cognized,
sn35.95:10.3: then, Māluṅkyaputta, there is no you in connection with that.
sn35.95:10.4: When there is no you in connection with that,
sn35.95:10.6: When there is no you there,
sn35.95:10.7: you are neither here nor yonder nor between the two.
sn35.95:10.8: This, just this, is the end of stress.”
sn35.95:11.1: “I understand in detail, lord, the meaning of what the Blessed One has said in brief:
sn35.95:13.2: born of the form, grow numerous,
sn35.95:13.6: one is said to be far from unbinding.
sn35.95:23.1: One’s feelings, born of the idea,
sn35.95:23.6: one is said to be far from unbinding.
sn35.95:24.4: and doesn’t remain fastened on it.
sn35.95:25.1: While one is seeing a form
sn35.95:25.3: it falls away and doesn’t accumulate.
sn35.95:25.6: one is said to be in the presence of unbinding.
sn35.95:34.4: and doesn’t remain fastened on it.
sn35.95:35.1: While one is knowing an idea
sn35.95:35.3: it falls away and doesn’t accumulate.
sn35.95:35.6: one is said to be in the presence of unbinding.
sn35.95:36.1: “It’s in this way, lord, that I understand in detail the meaning of what the Blessed One said in brief.”
sn35.95:36.3: It’s good that you understand in detail this way the meaning of what I said in brief.”
sn35.95:41.1: “It’s in this way, Māluṅkyaputta, that the meaning of what I said in brief should be regarded in detail.”
sn35.95:42.1: Then Ven. Māluṅkyaputta, having been admonished by the admonishment from the Blessed One, got up from his seat and bowed down to the Blessed One, circled around him, keeping the Blessed One to his right side, and left.
sn35.95:42.2: Then, dwelling alone, secluded, heedful, ardent, & resolute, he in no long time entered & remained in the supreme goal of the holy life for which clansmen rightly go forth from home into homelessness, directly knowing & realizing it for himself in the here & now.
sn35.95:42.3: He knew: “Birth is ended, the holy life fulfilled, the task done. There is nothing further for the sake of this world.”
sn35.95:42.4: And thus Ven. Māluṅkyaputta became another one of the arahants.
